summary: The presence of concurrent humeral Hill-Sachs lesions and glenoidal bone deficiency is common in patients with chronic anterior post-traumatic shoulder dislocations. This mutual relationship—where mechanical forces cause one defect leading directly to another—is clinically significant for assessing stability risk and planning joint-preserving surgery such as arthroplasty or osteochondral grafts.
